item.title: Eli Katoa's NRL future uncertain after head knocks, Melbourne Storm coach Craig Bellamy says
item.description: Melbourne Storm coach Craig Bellamy says it is too early to know if superstar back rower Eli Katoa will get back on the field in 2027 or at all.

item.title: Hezbollah serves as 'pretext' for Israeli attacks, Lebanon's Justice Minister Adel Nassar says
item.description: Lebanon's justice minister accuses the Iranian-backed militant group Hezbollah of effectively giving Israel an excuse to attack the country amid threats of an Israeli occupation of the south.

item.title: Regional WA ambulance volunteers receive 'unfathomable' donation from patient
item.description: A volunteer ambulance service in regional WA has received a donation from a thankful patient of more than $280,000, allowing it to purchase a new ambulance.

item.title: Donald Trump's Greenland threat likely to salvage Danish PM's political future
item.description: When Donald Trump threatened to annex Greenland, Denmark leader Mette Frederiksen was flailing in the polls. Having stood up to the US president, she looks to have salvaged her political career.

item.title: Donald Trump's backtrack illuminates Iran's leverage in an asymmetrical war
item.description: In the wake of Donald Trump's backtrack, Iran realises more clearly than ever that it wields a potent new weapon on the global stage.

item.title: Company that built highly criticised BOM website wins $16m contract for new site
item.description: The company responsible for the highly criticised BOM website wins a $16 million tender to build a new climate data website for the government.

item.title: Disability and chronic pain made motherhood isolating for Megan
item.description: Megan Blin says her chronic pain made it difficult to do things other first-time mums could.

item.title: Japan urges public not to panic-buy toilet paper amid Iran war
item.description: Japan's government releases a statement assuring people there is no issue with supply and no need to stock up, despite "reports and social media posts regarding the distribution of toilet paper".

item.title: Influencer Stacey Warnecke's 'birthkeeper' may be compelled to give evidence at inquest, court hears
item.description: A coroner investigating the death of nutrition influencer Stacey Warnecke after a freebirth may compel her support worker to appear at the inquest.

item.title: Iran shows Europe 'in reach' with longest-range ballistic missile launch
item.description: An attempted attack on a US-UK military base in the Indian Ocean by Iran shows Tehran can launch missiles as far as 4,000km, raising concerns European cities are in reach.

item.title: Beef and lamb exporters label EU free trade agreement 'simply appalling'
item.description: The National Farmers' Federation says the deal will leave farmers without meaningful access to the EU market.

item.title: Georgia Voll hits Australia's third fastest T20 ton in victory over West Indies
item.description: Georgia Voll puts her name alongside the likes of Alyssa Healy and Meg Lanning after smashing a 52-ball hundred in Australia's win over West Indies.

item.title: Pastoral company fined record $45,000 for Queensland biosecurity breaches
item.description: A pastoral company has been hit with a record $45,000 fine after moving thousands of cattle across state lines without proper tracking. Authorities say the penalty sends a clear warning.

item.title: Longest-running abalone farm in the Southern Hemisphere to shut due to harmful algal bloom
item.description: Fifteen permanent jobs will be lost on Kangaroo Island with the closure of Yumbah Aquaculture's abalone farm.

item.title: Audiences consider The Diary of Anne Frank an 'uplifting' story arriving in Sydney at the right time
item.description: While considering it a "big responsibility" bringing the theatre production to Sydney at this time, actress Chloe-Jean Vincent says Anne Frank's story makes theatregoers feel "inspired and so uplifted".

item.title: Talia Gibson's dream US run ended by world number two Elena Rybakina in Miami Open
item.description: Aussie Talia Gibson will leave the US full of pride and confidence despite falling to Elena Rybakina in the fourth round of the Miami Open.

item.title: Outback town of Marree receives anonymous donation of supplies after being cut off by floodwater
item.description: An anonymous donor funded two planes full of supplies for the outback town of Marree, near Kati Thanda-Lake Eyre, after it was cut off by flooding.

item.title: How Bron Lewis went from dingy bars to hosting a MICF gala
item.description: The crimson-haired comedian has gone from tiny open mics to the biggest stage in Melbourne in just a few short years.

item.title: Proposed Aboriginal land rights law change limits unused Crown land access, councils say
item.description: Aboriginal councils who have fought for decades to secure ancestral land are concerned proposed law reform puts rights at risk.

item.title: EU leader urges democracies to band together in 'upside down' world
item.description: The Europe Commission president declares that Europe and Australia must rearm and decarbonise as they try to break economic and energy dependencies on China and Russia.

item.title: Iranians celebrate ancient Nowruz despite repression and war with US, Israel
item.description: Amid war and repression, Iranians cling to hope with the celebration of Nowruz, the pre-Islamic tradition symbolising renewal.

item.title: Organised criminals increasingly working with foreign 'despots and dictators', AFP boss warns
item.description: The Australian Federal Police is warning that foreign intelligence agencies are increasingly working with locally-based organised criminals to carry out "serious crimes", including terrorism, within Australia.

item.title: Tropical Cyclone Narelle to re-form off northern WA coast
item.description: Coastal communities in the Kimberley region will be affected in the next 24 hours as Tropical Cyclone Narelle, which had been downgraded to a low, intensifies back to cyclone level.

item.title: Oil price rises, Australian dollar falls as Strait of Hormuz remains effectively shut
item.description: A key factor for shares, bonds, commodities and currencies remains consistently unchanged — the Strait of Hormuz is still effectively closed.

item.title: Australia's diesel standards lowered for six months as hundreds of petrol stations run out of certain fuels
item.description: Australia is temporarily lowering standards for diesel to allow more supply into the domestic market as service stations across the country continue to experience localised shortages.

item.title: Alex Robertson says Socceroos allegiance was never in doubt as he pushes for World Cup selection
item.description: Alex Robertson says his allegiance to the Socceroos was never in doubt, as he pushes to earn selection in Tony Popovic's World Cup squad.

item.title: Reducing 'just in case' purchases can help avoid empty shelves and fuel bowsers
item.description: If you've topped up your tank at a petrol station recently did it feel like you were "panic buying"?

item.title: Brazilian man Marcelo Peres-Colhado, accused of being part of car theft syndicate, denied bail
item.description: The prosecution argued the man was in Australia on an expired visa and could face deportation if granted bail.

item.title: Labor caucus chooses new cabinet ministers as Liberals pledge election review
item.description: As SA Liberal leader Ashton Hurn pledged to review the weekend's catastrophic defeat for her party, the Labor caucus met for the first time since its resounding win, with the premier hitting back at remarks made by One Nation's Pauline Hanson.

item.title: Alleged petrol station thief Glenn McCagh in court after servo worker seriously injured in hit-and-run
item.description: An alleged fuel thief accused of seriously injuring a Perth petrol station worker in a hit-and-run is told he will likely go to prison if convicted of the "extremely serious" charges.

item.title: Tropical Cyclone Narelle set to impact Perth over weekend, with heavy rainfall expected
item.description: Tropical Cyclone Narelle is set to impact Perth and towns north of the city over the weekend with heavy rainfall expected.

item.title: Brisbane Broncos 'surprised' as Ben Te'o resigns after dispute with coach Michael Maguire
item.description: Brisbane defensive coach Ben Te'o, the mastermind behind the scrambling defence in the 2025 grand final win, hands in his resignation.

item.title: Tuesday finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

item.title: Gold Coast Suns's Bailey Humphrey fails at AFL tribunal to get dangerous tackle ban overturned
item.description: Young gun Bailey Humphrey will miss Gold Coast's next two matches through suspension, after the AFL tribunal rejects his arguments against a rough conduct ban.

item.title: Former defence leaders outline already-present fossil fuel dependence, climate disinformation threats
item.description: Australians are already deeply emersed in a global disinformation war about fossil fuels and climate change, former defence leaders say in an extensive new review.

item.title: Married at First Sight expert Mel Schilling dies, aged 54
item.description: Schilling's husband, Gareth Brisbane, took to social media on Tuesday night to reveal the news.

item.title: Israel plans to occupy southern Lebanon from the Blue Line to the Litani River
item.description: Israel's defence minister has announced plans for his country's military to take control of southern Lebanon, from the Israeli border — known as the Blue Line — to the Litani River.

item.title: Travel quiz, March 25: Do you have the stamina to complete this trip?
item.description: Which country has the dong as its currency? And where would you dine on tteokbokki?

item.title: The Kates, Kate McLennan and Kate McCartney, take crime comedy Deadloch to Darwin
item.description: Kate McLennan and Kate McCartney (Get Krack!n) set out to make a "funny Broadchurch" with crime comedy Deadloch. Now, with fans around the world, it's back for season two.

item.title: Did AI really take their jobs?
item.description: As big tech companies sack staff and say AI will pick up the slack, workers are left to grapple with what's really going on.

item.title: NDIS participants work more, feel better after accessing scheme, report finds
item.description: New analysis by the e61 Institute, which tracks NDIS participants over more than a decade, finds substantial improvements to their workforce participation and self-reported energy levels within four years of accessing the taxpayer-funded scheme.

item.title: Home Affairs Minister Tony Burke admits luck — not a plan — saved lives in Perth Australia Day failed bomb attack
item.description: The home affairs minister says Australia was "so lucky" to avoid mass casualties during the attempted Australia Day bomb attack in Perth and admits the nation was slow to recognise the gravity of the alleged terrorist act.

item.title: Waste collectors warn bin services may stop if diesel not found urgently
item.description: Waste collectors say they are struggling to acquire diesel and many are operating at "unsustainable" losses, putting the collection of household bins and commercial waste under threat.

item.title: Donald Trump says Iran sent US 'big present' as Pentagon readies thousands more troops for Middle East
item.description: The US president suggests a mystery gift "worth a tremendous amount" is aiding diplomatic efforts to end the war, and comes as he reportedly prepares to send a major airborne force to the region.

item.title: The Malinauskas government's second term could be harder than its first
item.description: Despite holding an overwhelming majority in parliament, the Malinauskas government faces no shortage of policy and political challenges to navigate in its second term.

item.title: What you need to know about Iran today, with Matthew Doran
item.description: The focus of the Middle East war shifts to Lebanon as Israel plans to occupy southern parts of the country.

item.title: E-bike changes coming to NSW include reduced power and proposed age limit
item.description: The NSW government has recently announced staged reforms to improve safety for e-bike riders and pedestrians. This is what it'll mean for the community.

item.title: Wine growers fear smoke taint will spoil Victorian harvest as planned burns begin
item.description: Just months after devastating bushfires, wine growers are concerned planned burns in regional Victoria pose a new risk to their grapes.

item.title: Tropical Cyclone Narelle re-forms off WA, expected to reach category four
item.description: Having cut a swathe across northern Australia over the past week, Tropical Cyclone Narelle is now threatening coastal communities in Western Australia.

item.title: Russia fires nearly 400 drones in deadly attack on Ukraine, with UNESCO site targeted
item.description: Three were killed and 30 wounded in a rare daytime attack, including on the city of Lviv, while four have been killed in an overnight bombardment.

item.title: Labor says it is 'way too soon' to talk about fuel rationing — as it happened
item.description: The government says it is "too soon" to be talking about fuel rationing as the war in the Middle East disrupts supply.

item.title: Alleged northern NSW bank robber arrested after 30 years on the run
item.description: A man wanted by police over two bank robberies in NSW almost 30 years ago has been arrested and charged.

item.title: Three people killed, two injured in crash involving car and truck near Birdwood
item.description: The sedan driver had life-threatening injuries and a fourth passenger was seriously injured.

item.title: Football Australia vows high-quality Matildas opponents in lead-up to 2027 Women's World Cup
item.description: After the highs of their run to the Asian Cup final, in just over two weeks the Matildas travel to Kenya to face teams ranked outside the top 100.

item.title: Third man charged over the alleged abduction and murder of Chris Baghsarian
item.description: Police allege the 23-year-old man was a "major player" in organising the abduction plot.

item.title: Live: Australian share market rises ahead of CPI data release
item.description: Australian shares have opened higher, after a volatile session on Wall Street where investors swayed between fears of rising oil prices and hopes for a resolution to the US-Israeli war on Iran. Follow the latest updates in our live blog.

item.title: Mo Salah announces he will leave Liverpool at end of EPL season
item.description: Mo Salah announces he is leaving Liverpool after almost a decade of starring for the EPL giants.

item.title: Woman dies after being struck by truck in North West Queensland
item.description: A woman dies after being hit by a truck on the Barkly Highway in North West Queensland.

item.title: Cacti confiscated from home garden over fears of another 'prickly pear' disaster
item.description: An opal miner's garden plants have been destroyed after it was identified as a prohibited plant, potentially as invasive as one of Australia's most notorious plants.

item.title: Trump claims to have received 'very big present' from Iran
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Federal and state governments announce $2b bailout for Rio Tinto's Boyne aluminium smelter
item.description: Rio Tinto, the federal and state governments have struck a landmark partnership to secure the long-term future of the Boyne aluminium smelter.

item.title: Michael Atherton, Michael Vaughan and Jonny Bairstow question England's Ashes review
item.description: Former England captains and current player Jonny Bairstow question the findings of an internal review into a disastrous Ashes campaign, which saw the team's leaders maintain their positions.

item.title: Cyclone Narelle's trajectory puts Western Australia on hit list
item.description: Tropical Cyclone Narelle is now barrelling towards Western Australia. Could this unusual cyclone make it all the way to Perth, and how does climate change affect it?

item.title: WA Opposition Leader Basil Zempilas 'not fearful' of One Nation's rise ahead of 2029 WA election
item.description: Basil Zempilas is "not fearful" of the soaring rise of Pauline Hanson's One Nation in the west, but acknowledges his WA Liberals will need to decide whether to direct preference votes to the controversial party or distance themselves.

item.title: Cyclone Narelle tracker map: Where is it forecast to cross WA coast?
item.description: Track the path of Tropical Cyclone Narelle as it heads towards the WA coast.

item.title: Major facelift proposed for Mount Gambier's sunken Umpherston Sinkhole garden
item.description: The Mount Gambier council is asking for feedback on a significant change to the regional city's beloved sunken garden.

item.title: Tom Gleeson, Aaron Chen, Paul McDermott and more on 40 years of the Melbourne Comedy Festival
item.description: The very first Melbourne International Comedy Festival launched 40 years ago, and while it's kept its scrappy charm, things look pretty different today.

item.title: Couples on sleeping separately and if it's helped — or hindered — their relationships
item.description: Snoring, shift work, parenting and health — you shared the many reasons why you no longer sleep in the same bed as your partner, and whether it's been a positive move for sleep and the relationship.

item.title: Bulk carrier that escaped Iranian blockade of Strait of Hormuz due to arrive in Australia
item.description: The Japanese-owned Iron Maiden will be the first known ship to dock in Australia after it was stranded in the Persian Gulf when the US and Israel began bombing Iran on February 28.

item.title: Jetstar reduces flights between New Zealand and Australia due to rising jet fuel prices
item.description: Jetstar announces it is reducing flights between Australia and New Zealand due to the rising cost of jet fuel as a result of the war in the Middle East.

item.title: Drone baiting aims to eradicate infestation of house mice from remote WA island
item.description: Scientists are using "world-first" drone-baiting technology to target thousands of house mice in an effort to bring migratory seabirds back from the brink on Browse Island off Western Australia's north.

item.title: Married at First Sight expert Mel Schilling remembered as 'amazing' and 'radiant' after bowel cancer death
item.description: Mel Schilling became one of Australian TV's most recognised relationship experts, but who was she, and what led to her death?

item.title: Government talks down fuel rationing, as Chalmers concedes 5 per cent inflation scenario 'conservative'
item.description: The government has requested new Treasury modelling for "more challenging circumstances" stemming from the Middle East war, admitting that the economic scenarios released last week "look pretty conservative now".

item.title: 25yo man dies in NT police custody after arrest and detainment in police car cage in Darwin
item.description: A 25-year-old Indigenous man has died in police custody in Darwin's northern suburbs overnight, with police saying he was "arrested without incident".

item.title: Inflation cooled slightly in February before energy price rise caused by Iran war
item.description: The latest monthly data does not capture the recent rise in energy prices caused by the war in the Middle East, which began on February 28.

item.title: Public servant Nadine Marie Smith allegedly helped intimate partner skip social housing waitlist
item.description: A 55-year-old public servant is accused of illegally offering a social housing tenancy to her intimate partner, allowing them to skip a large number of applicants who were in the queue for a home.

item.title: Danish election bruises Mette Frederiksen's Social Democrats amid fights over immigration and Greenland
item.description: Danish Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen's Social Democrats look set to suffer the party's worst election outcome in more than a century.

item.title: OpenAI pulls plug on AI video app Sora that sparked deepfake concerns
item.description: Sora was released in September by the company behind ChatGPT and soon went viral as a place to share short-form videos generated by artificial intelligence, but it also raised alarms.

item.title: Hong Kong police arrest bookstore owner and staff for allegedly selling Jimmy Lai biography, broadcaster reports
item.description: Hong Kong police have arrested a bookstore owner and three shopkeepers for allegedly selling "seditious" publications.

item.title: Wallabies flyhalf Tom Lynagh returns for Reds, Zac Lomax joins Western Force teammates
item.description: The Queensland Reds are attempting to achieve their first Super Rugby win in Wellington since 1998, and their maiden win at a ground known as the Cake Tin.

item.title: Democrats flip seat in Florida district home to Donald Trump's Mar-a-Lago resort
item.description: Democrats celebrate the victory as the latest sign voters are turning against Donald Trump and Republicans ahead of November's midterm elections.

item.title: Shorter showers, reduced flights and no street lights among measures to address Asia's energy crisis
item.description: Countries across Asia are moving to protect their energy supplies after Iran effectively blockaded the crucial Strait of Hormuz passage in response to the United States and Israel's attacks.

item.title: South Australian Premier Peter Malinauskas announces new cabinet ministers in major reshuffle
item.description: South Australian Premier Peter Malinauskas has unveiled his new-look second-term cabinet. Among the changes, Chris Picton has been moved out of the health portfolio and replaced by former education minister Blair Boyer.

item.title: Russia fires nearly 400 drones at Ukraine with signs its spring offensive has begun
item.description: Russia's anticipated annual spring offensive appears to have started, with a major drone assault on civilian areas coinciding with multiple attempts to break through frontline Ukrainian defences.

item.title: Questions over deal between AI company Firmus and Tasmania's power generator
item.description: Questions remain about the deal struck to supply power to an AI company in Tasmania's north, not long after a request to supply another manufacturer was rejected.

item.title: F1 team principal roles dominate headlines, futures of Adrian Newey and Jonathan Wheatley up in the air
item.description: Formula 1 has produced drama on and off the track in the early stages of the 2026 season, with focus squarely on the team principal roles of two teams.

item.title: Shepparton man spared jail after leaving child in cardboard box during car theft
item.description: A 32-year-old man says he is "ashamed" of his actions after he left a child in a cardboard box in a regional Victorian car park while stealing the vehicle the toddler was in.

item.title: Foreign Minister Penny Wong warns Israel against south Lebanon occupation
item.description: Australia warns Israel against occupying parts of Lebanon, urging it to respect international law after the displacement of more than 1 million people.

item.title: Iranian visa holders temporarily barred from travelling to Australia
item.description: Home Affairs Minister Tony Burke says the war in Iran increases the risk some Iranians may not be able to leave Australia when their visa expires.

item.title: NASA pivots to moon base, nuclear propulsion in expanded multi-billion-dollar program
item.description: NASA has cancelled plans to deploy a space station in lunar orbit and will instead build a $28.7 billion base on the moon's surface.

item.title: Australian shares soar on Middle East ceasefire hopes and softer inflation figures
item.description: The local share market has its best day in almost a year after reports the US is seeking a ceasefire with Iran buoyed investor sentiment, and monthly inflation was softer than expected.

item.title: Kyle Sandilands on Enough Rope
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Will Kyle Sandilands really sue radio network ARN?
item.description: There's an $88 million question doing the rounds of legal and media circles, after the catastrophic falling-out between radio shock jocks Kyle and Jackie O and their former radio network ARN.

item.title: NSW forestry industry calls for secure future amid interstate shutdowns
item.description: The NSW forestry industry is calling for secure access to timber as the proposed Great Koala National Park locks up resources and recent closures of Victorian and Western Australian sectors raise fears for the future of native logging.

item.title: From the Gulf War of the 1990s to the 2020 pandemic, global crises generally follow a predictable path
item.description: If the mass uncertainty sparked by the military escalation in Iran is making you feel anxious, you are not alone — but there is some calming news for us all.

item.title: What we know and don't know about the Iran war negotiations
item.description: US President Donald Trump's surprising claim that talks with Iran were yielding great progress only raised more confusion over a war whose goals were already unclear.

item.title: This Mediterranean-style diet could keep your brain sharp as you age
item.description: The food choices we make over decades — not just in later life, but across adulthood — may quietly shape the health of our brains in ways that only become visible much later.

item.title: Queensland rising in battle against WA and SA for next AFL heartland
item.description: The question of who loves footy the most has sparked countless hours of debate over the past century, and Queensland is staking a major claim.

item.title: ABC Shakespeare quiz, parteth the seceund: Test thine knowledge of his plays once more
item.description: Is this a quiz which you see before you? We're back with another 10 questions to test your knowledge of William Shakespeare's plays.

item.title: Family launches palliative care foundation after 33-year-old son's final hospital experience
item.description: Ryan Bowman fought through congenital heart defects and cancer, but his family says his final hospital visit led to some frightening final hours.

item.title: From crafting to surfing, regional Victorians dive into hobbies to make new friends
item.description: From crafting to surfing, people of all ages are experimenting with new hobbies to meet pals in regional Victoria.

item.title: The Gold Coast has transformed over 50 years. Its growth debate hasn't
item.description: Fifty years after fears it would be a "concrete jungle" first surfaced, the Gold Coast is again grappling with the same question: how to grow without losing its identity.

item.title: Seasons clash as a polar blast joins summer cyclone on Australia's weather map
item.description: A clash of seasons is developing on Australia's weather map this week as a tropical cyclone tracks towards the WA west coast and a polar blast with thunderstorms forms over the south-east.

item.title: Former Brisbane Lion Leigh Ryswyk becomes first openly gay AFL player
item.description: Former Brisbane Lions player Leigh Ryswyk has become the first openly gay AFL player, past or present.

item.title: Our interest in electric vehicles has grown due to oil price spikes. And it's likely to remain
item.description: The clear signal this trend data sends is that Australians are a pragmatic lot. If using an EV might save them money, then they are interested.

item.title: What you need to know about Iran today, with Matthew Doran
item.description: Both the Trump administration and the Iranian regime have their own unique ways of expressing themselves — and both are prone to hyperbole, says Mark Doran in Beirut.

item.title: White House doubles down on threat against Iran
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: US says Iran seeking 'exit ramp' despite mixed ceasefire signals
item.description: The White House says talks with Iran aimed at ending the conflict have not stalled, despite conflicting reports on Iran's response to a 15-point ceasefire proposal.

item.title: South Australia seek successive Sheffield Shield titles in final against Victoria
item.description: Victory in this year's Sheffield Shield final over Victoria will be especially sweet for South Australia, with coach Ryan Harris saying their path to the title decider this time around has been challenging.

item.title: Savannah Guthrie in NBC News interview appeals for help finding missing mother
item.description: It is the Today show host's first interview since her mother Nancy was reported missing from her Arizona home on February 1.

item.title: Stephen Colbert to co‑write new Lord of the Rings film after late-night show ends
item.description: Talk show host Stephen Colbert, famous devotee to JRR Tolkien's Middle Earth, is co-writing a Lord of the Rings movie with his son.

item.title: WA coast 'pummelled' as Cyclone Narelle could reach category five on approach
item.description: Authorities in Western Australia say they are preparing for weather not seen since 2021, when a deadly cyclone flattened buildings in a town unaccustomed to such extreme conditions.

item.title: Israel says it has killed Iran's navy commander, Trump says Tehran 'begging' for a deal
item.description: The US-Israeli campaign has hit Iran's military and government hard, killing top leaders and striking scores of targets, but Iran continues to fire missiles.

item.title: NFL confirms date and time for LA Rams versus San Francisco 49ers at the MCG
item.description: The first-ever regular-season NFL game played in Australia has had its date confirmed, with the Melbourne Cricket Ground to host the historic match in the middle of the AFL finals series.

item.title: Adelaide Crows coach Matthew Nicks remains positive as understrength side faces Cats in Geelong
item.description: Matthew Nicks is imploring injury-hit Adelaide to "have a real crack" at ending the Crows' long-standing hoodoo in Geelong.

item.title: Sea the 'greatest risk and biggest asset' for beachside residents on Victoria's Bellarine and Barwon coasts
item.description: For residents of Victoria's Bellarine Peninsula like Vicki, who is facing the impacts of climate change-induced erosion and inundation, the water near their doors is both their biggest asset and greatest risk.

item.title: Law reform review calls for overhaul of WA's 'strictest' guardianship secrecy laws
item.description: People living under a guardianship arrangement in WA can't publicly speak up, but a review says this has to change.

item.title: PM calls second emergency national cabinet meeting over fuel crisis
item.description: States and territory leaders will convene next week to further coordinate the national response to the fuel crisis as the fallout from the Iran war continues to escalate.

item.title: Sophie Edwards wins national individual pursuit title, eyes Commonwealth Games while juggling medical degree
item.description: Sophie Edwards somehow manages to balance completing a medical degree with bike racing at the top level on both the road and the track. And it seems she's getting better than ever.

item.title: Sheffield Shield final: Alex Carey unbeaten as South Australia's top order rocked by Victoria before rain
item.description: Victoria sent South Australia in to bat on day one of the Sheffield Shield final and claimed three wickets before rain stopped play.

item.title: Controversial Melbourne nightclub Love Machine destroyed by fire
item.description: Victoria Police are investigating a suspicious fire that tore through a controversial Melbourne nightclub.

item.title: Hannah Gadsby, Rove McManus and Luke Heggie recommend comedians to see at the comedy festival
item.description: Comedy season is in full swing, with festivals across the country hosting the best acts from Australia and the world. We asked our favourites to share their recommendations.

item.title: TGA proposes reforms to sunscreen regulation including new testing technologies
item.description: The proposals include a number of changes to SPF testing requirements, oversight of the labs that do SPF testing, and the way SPF is labelled — reforms that would overhaul the sunscreen industry.

item.title: Over 300 petrol stations out of diesel, PM says 'nowhere is immune' to global crisis — as it happened
item.description: During Question Time, Anthony Albanese faced a grilling on the impacts of war in the Middle East.

item.title: Pope makes Australian Anthony Randazzo an archbishop, appoints him to senior Vatican role
item.description: Anthony Randazzo, the bishop of Broken Bay, which since 2019 covers Sydney's northern regions and the central coast, receives the personal title of archbishop and is named as Prefect of the Dicastery for Legislative Texts.

item.title: ASX closes slightly down while NAB plans hundreds of job cuts — as it happened
item.description: The ASX 200 has closed slightly down as Iran's foreign minister says the country will not negotiate with the US on a ceasefire plan. Brent crude remains above $US102 a barrel.

item.title: Leigh Ryswyk says reaction to being first openly gay AFL player 'overwhelming'
item.description: Former Brisbane Lions player Leigh Ryswyk says the support he received after becoming the first AFL player to come out as gay has "blown me away".

item.title: Deklin Donnelly fronts court over alleged abduction and murder of Chris Baghsarian
item.description: The man who police have alleged to be a "major player" in the mistaken identity kidnapping and murder of a Sydney grandfather, Deklin Donnelly, has fronted court for the first time.

item.title: Federal government urges rise in minimum wage above rate of inflation
item.description: In its submission to the Fair Work Commission's annual wage review, the government again calls for the lowest-paid workers to not go backwards.

item.title: AFL concussion class action expands to more than 100 former players and 11 clubs
item.description: Eight more footballers have joined a multimillion-dollar concussion class action against the AFL, tipping the total to over 100 former players who all claim they have been left brain-damaged by the game.

item.title: After a lopsided and uncertain start to the season, the NRL risks losing itself in a maze of set restarts
item.description: After three rounds of the NRL season the increase in set restarts has left coaches, players and fans alike more confused than ever about the specifics behind rugby league's most controversial rule change.

item.title: AFL reveals new illicit drug policy that Andrew Dillon says is 'most stringent in Australian sport'
item.description: The AFL says its new illicit drugs policy, which includes biannual hair-testing of all players, is the most stringent in Australian sport.

item.title: SA premier says regional areas will be prioritised for fuel deliveries amid global oil crisis
item.description: South Australia's premier has assured farmers battling with soaring diesel prices that regional areas will be first to receive fuel deliveries.

item.title: AI detecting crocodiles in Queensland waterways
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: AI crocodile detection trials begin in north Queensland
item.description: Researchers are trialling artificial intelligence to detect crocodiles in north Queensland waterways, but its success depends on human training to ensure accuracy.

item.title: Federal government's ban on Iranian tourists to block 7,000 visa holders from Australia
item.description: Thousands of Iranian travellers have now been barred from entering Australia under a government ban, prompting criticism by refugee advocates and some crossbenchers.

item.title: Amateur lobster fisher escapes attack by 3.5m great white shark off Cape Jaffa
item.description: A fisher known as "Lucky Luke" to his mates lives up to his nickname after surviving a great white shark dragging him along the ocean floor while lobster fishing off the South Australian coast.

item.title: States to seek standardised reporting of fuel supply levels at national cabinet meeting
item.description: State and territory leaders are also expected to push for a national approach to any further measures, including fuel rationing, if supply were to run short.

item.title: Queensland man charged with foreign incursion offence over alleged plans to fight in Myanmar civil war
item.description: Police allege Attilio Ivor Filip Dallavalle travelled to Thailand in January with plans to travel onward to Myanmar to engage in "hostile activities" with an armed group engaged in the ongoing conflict.

item.title: Sydney private school teacher accused by police of grooming 14-year-old girl
item.description: A casual teacher at Newington College has been charged with allegedly encouraging a 14-year-old girl to send sexually explicit images for money.

item.title: Centre for Disease Control issues health warning after outbreak of respiratory diphtheria in NT
item.description: The Northern Territory's health department has declared an outbreak of respiratory diphtheria in the Top End.

item.title: Socceroos approaching last chance saloon, with World Cup dreams in the balance
item.description: The Socceroos' upcoming games against Cameroon and Curaçao could make or shatter World Cup dreams, and Socceroos legend Mark Schwarzer has backed the players, and coach Tony Popovic, to deliver come tournament time.

item.title: Germany's defence minister says Donald Trump has 'no exit strategy' in Iran
item.description: Boris Pistorius says Germany will only help to secure the Strait of Hormuz once the US and Iran strike a ceasefire deal.

item.title: Risk of vegetable price rise in response to spiralling fuel and fertilizer costs
item.description: A snap survey by AUSVEG reveals 27 per cent of Australian vegetable growers have already reduced planting, while some will not harvest current crops because rising costs make it unviable.

item.title: Mount Alexander shire urges government to force fossil fuel companies to fund climate disaster recovery
item.description: A Victorian council still cleaning up after a bushfire that wiped out more than 50 homes and businesses wants fossil fuel companies to pay for climate disaster recovery.

item.title: Gold Coast mayor yet to decide if he'll vote on Trump tower after gifts
item.description: Tom Tate has been pressed on whether he would exclude himself from chambers if the Trump tower came to a vote, after he received gifts from the organisation.

item.title: Relief as Katherine River peaks without further damage, remote evacuees head home
item.description: Major flooding has eased along the Katherine River and the town has been spared further damage, while surrounding remote communities are starting to recover.

item.title: Unpacking the social media addiction ruling against Meta and YouTube, and what it means for users
item.description: A US jury has found Instagram and YouTube were designed to be addictive to young users, in a landmark trial experts say could influence thousands of similar lawsuits.

item.title: Funding for Macquarie Point precinct inches forward after key plans approved by federal government
item.description: Federal funding for Hobart's waterfront redevelopment and stadium has reached a key milestone, with the Australian government accepting the site's master and housing plans.

item.title: Superintendent Sachin Sharma sues NT Police for demotion 'because of race'
item.description: A former NT Police Acting Assistant Commissioner has claimed his career is in tatters after he was overlooked for promotion and demoted for "reasons including" his race.

item.title: Fuel dominates politics, Hanson confused over Rinehart flight questions
item.description: The ongoing fuel crisis dominated politics this week but a probe into the National Anti-Corruption Commission was also announced and Pauline Hanson was grilled about accepting free flights from Gina Rinehart.

item.title: Why One Nation is not winning many lower house seats in the SA election despite its high vote
item.description: One Nation has received more than 20 per cent of the primary vote in South Australia, and so far, its lower house seat count remains slight. Why is that, and why are onlookers so interested in the result?

item.title: Vision of crane collapse in Dubbo
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Sidon is facing a new wave of displaced residents from Lebanon's south as Israel's offensive continues
item.description: Southern Lebanon has a "very long history" of conflict with Israel, and many of its residents remember past wars clearly. But they say the latest incursion feels different.

item.title: More charges laid against man accused of posing as Disney casting agent to procure sexual favours
item.description: Police have laid 45 more charges dating back to 2014 against a Sydney man accused of posing as a Disney casting agent and offering movie opportunities for sex acts, with a magistrate describing it as "some of the most manipulative behaviour I've seen".

item.title: The One Nation vote in SA
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Last-touch non-call labelled 'farce' as Geelong edges Adelaide in thriller
item.description: Geelong continues its streak over Adelaide at Kardinia Park, as one of the AFL's off-season rule changes is put squarely in the spotlight for the wrong reasons.

item.title: Sixteen-year-old girl the fifth person charged over death of Aidan Becker at Mernda railway station
item.description: A 16-year-old girl is the fifth person to be charged following the death of a 22-year-old man at a train station in Melbourne's outer north earlier this month.

item.title: Thursday finance with Daniel Ziffer
item.description: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

item.title: Cherry-Evans has last laugh as Sea Eagles slump to third-straight defeat
item.description: Daly Cherry-Evans is roundly booed on his return to Brookvale after his off-season switch from the Sea Eagles to the Roosters, as the tricolours dominate territory to claim 33-16 victory.

item.title: At 104, George Franklin is months away from becoming the world's oldest conductor
item.description: WA musician George Franklin has already achieved a milestone by turning 104. He could soon garner another: the record for being the world's oldest band conductor.

item.title: Rising fuel prices drive surge in demand for emergency support in Canberra
item.description: Rising petrol and diesel prices are driving Canberra families to seek emergency assistance and forcing small businesses to consider whether to pass on higher costs to customers.

item.title: Qantas ramps up services to Europe as travellers avoid Middle East stopovers
item.description: Qantas will increase capacity on flights between Australia and Europe after Easter, which experts say may see the "kangaroo route" evolve.

item.title: Vision of crane collapse in Dubbo
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Severe storm causes thousands of power outages and hundreds calls for help in Sydney
item.description: Fire and Rescue NSW says there has been more than 400 calls for help, including one incident where a tree fell on a house trapping the occupant.

item.title: International Olympic Committee bans transgender athletes from women's events
item.description: Under changes announced by the International Olympic Committee, the gender of athletes will be determined by a one-time gene-screening test.

item.title: Why comedian Nazeem Hussain laughs about the things that make him want to cry
item.description: For comedian Nazeem Hussain, the best way to respond to war, discrimination and racism is to laugh about it. And he's built his whole career on this special brand of risky, outrageous comedy.

item.title: Former Venezuelan president Nicolás Maduro appears in New York court after January capture
item.description: Former Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro and his wife return to the New York courtroom after being captured by US forces in January.

item.title: Eight-fold increase in EPA odour complaints in Melbourne's west linked to small number of businesses
item.description: Adam Pattison spent two years doing up his backyard to enjoy outdoor barbecues, but says "sickening" smells kill the vibe five times a week.

item.title: Live: 'Monster' Cyclone Narelle to cross WA coast later today
item.description: The storm, which has already made landfall in Far North Queensland and the Northern Territory, is now impacting the west Pilbara coast.

item.title: Review of Tasmania's climate laws finds net zero status could be 'temporary'
item.description: Tasmania loves to boast about its net zero emissions. But a new report has found that status could be "temporary" if further actions are not taken.

item.title: Weekly news quiz: Jet fuel shortages, headbutting whales and Donald Trump's social faux pas
item.description: It's been a big week in the news. Did you catch these stories?

item.title: Medical drama Grey's Anatomy marks 21 years of love, loss and disasters
item.description: Twenty-one years ago today, a group of interns walked into the fictional Seattle Grace Hospital to start their surgical careers. It was the start of the longest-running medical drama in US TV history.

item.title: Trump singles out Australia as he criticises allies on Iran war support
item.description: US President Donald Trump singles out Australia as a country he is surprised is unwilling to help secure the Strait of Hormuz during the war with Iran.

item.title: Fuel price spike drives multi-million-dollar blowouts on public works
item.description: Industry groups have warned some infrastructure projects could stall without additional government support to cover fuel-related costs.

item.title: Oil crisis revives talks of levy on plastic packaging to wean Australia off imports
item.description: The war in Iran renews the government's interest in reducing Australia's dependence on imported packaging, particularly oil-reliant plastic.

item.title: Albanese government struggles under 'stress test' posed by Middle East war
item.description: Fuel prices are the immediate issue for the Albanese government but it will also be under scrutiny for how it handles Australia's relationship with the United States and its volatile president.

item.title: Superannuation admin provider Grow Inc's financial trouble raises concerns for HESTA
item.description: Experts are concerned one of Australia's largest super funds has invested in a new admin company whose most recent financial report indicates it is insolvent.

item.title: Are the US and Iran about to reach a ceasefire deal?
item.description: Donald Trump says Iran wants a ceasefire deal. But Tehran says it's not even talking with the US. So what's going on and how likely is a ceasefire?

item.title: How 'traditional' gender roles are making a comeback in modern society
item.description: It turns out generation Z has some of the strongest traditional beliefs about gender roles. The revival of what many see as outdated gender norms is being attributed to "manosphere" content on social media.

item.title: Trump extends deadline for Iran to open the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: US President Donald Trump extends his deadline for Iran to open the Strait of Hormuz, saying he will hold off on bombing Iran's energy plants. Earlier, an Iranian official called US proposals for a ceasefire "one-sided and unfair". Follow live.

item.title: What you need to know about Iran today, with Matthew Doran
item.description: Donald Trump is now sceptical about the prospect of a peace deal soon. Middle East correspondent Matthew Doran has your daily update on the conflict.

item.title: Vanished: Six months of searching for Gus Lamont
item.description: Six months to the day since four-year-old Gus Lamont was reported missing from a vast outback station, many more questions than answers remain.

item.title: Advocates call for funding of transplant strategy to arrest declining organ donation rate
item.description: Vanessa Hujber did not think twice before donating her kidney to save the life of her daughter, Amelia. But Amelia was one of the lucky ones, with advocates calling for urgent action to arrest Australia's declining living organ donation rate.

item.title: Police to mark 40th anniversary of Melbourne's Russell Street bombing
item.description: The 1986 car bomb attack on Victoria Police headquarters in Melbourne claimed the life of 21-year-old constable Angela Taylor and injured more than 20 others.

item.title: Male victim-survivors of childhood sexual abuse overrepresented in suicide statistics, but seldom spoken about
item.description: Craig Hughes-Cashmore spent more than 20 years living with persistent suicidal thoughts, an experience that many share but rarely feel able to discuss.

item.title: Second alleged 'crew' charged over Bankstown woman Thi Kim Tran's kidnapping, murder
item.description: Further charges have been laid over the alleged kidnapping and murder of a western Sydney woman who police believe was targeted due to her husband's organised crime links.

item.title: Live: Marles defends Australia's Middle East contribution after Trump's comments
item.description: Deputy Prime Minister Richard Marles has defended Australia's contribution to the defence of Gulf states after US President Donald Trump's comments. Follow live.

item.title: Daly Cherry-Evans's return to Brookvale Oval a look into the past as Manly faces uncertain future
item.description: With Manly's season teetering on the edge, the Sea Eagles faithful welcomed former skipper Daly Cherry-Evans to Brookvale Oval in enemy colours for the first time. But it was the prodigal son who won the day.

item.title: Almost one in three eligible voters in Coober Pedy were unable to cast a ballot
item.description: Nearly one in three Coober Pedy voters, or more than 250 people, missed their chance to vote in the state election last week after the town was left without a polling booth on election day.

item.title: Katherine's worst flood in 28 years leaves survivors with disaster 'PTSD'
item.description: As it recovers from its worst flooding in decades, those living in the NT town of Katherine are trying to come to terms with the ongoing threat on their doorstep.

item.title: Donald Trump takes swipe at Australia over lack of support
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Live: ASX to drop following sea of red on Wall St
item.description: The ASX is set to fall at the open, following a sea of red on Wall Street. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

item.title: Mudgee Medical Centre secures six doctors to reopen books after 18 months
item.description: A medical centre in Central West NSW is accepting new patients for the first time in 18 months, after community support helped attract six new doctors.

item.title: Corporate watchdog ASIC expands in NT after investigation into Indigenous bank customer fees
item.description: The chair of the Australian Securities and Investments Commission says the expansion of the watchdog agency in Darwin will help the organisation better enforce regulations in remote communities.

item.title: Max Verstappen kicks Guardian F1 reporter out of Japanese Grand Prix press conference
item.description: Max Verstappen refuses to speak to media until Guardian journalist Giles Richards leaves because of a question he asked last year.

item.title: Petition calls for release of police video of Indigenous man's death in custody
item.description: Raylene Nixon says the public should be able to make their own minds up about what happened to her son in his final moments.

item.title: Tropical Cyclone Narelle impacts west Pilbara coast as a category four system
item.description: Residents on WA's West Pilbara coast are feeling the full force of Tropical Cyclone Narelle as it travels south as a category four system.

item.title: Exmouth Shire President describes 'terrifying' scenes as Tropical Cyclone Narelle approaches
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Wind power off Victoria's coast could reduce need for new transmission lines, report finds
item.description: Developing Victoria's wind power sector could save the state money and powerline infrastructure, while providing stability for Eastern Australia's power grid, a new report shows.
